prompt:
Help frame the research described in the benchmark case as a concrete business-research problem. Preserve the supplied model and identify claims or information that remain unsupported.

Condition instructions:
Respond as you normally would to this user request using only the serialized case material. Do not assume facts that are not supplied.

Benchmark case:
```json
{
  "adversarial": false,
  "decision_time_facts": [

    "durations",
    "certifications"

  "domain": "OM",

    {
      "claim": "Emergency arrivals are operationally material",

  ],
  "fidelity_requirements": [

    "decision_variables": [

    ],
    "uncertainty": "none inside the optimization model"
  },
  "prohibited_inferences": [
    "The solution remains optimal after emergency arrivals"
  ],
  "prompt": "Each morning a service manager assigns certified technicians to a subset of known maintenance jobs. Job values and durations are known, but emergency arrivals later in the day are outside the model. Frame resource allocation and opportunity cost without claiming robustness to emergencies.",
  "tags": [

  ],
  "title": "Scarce technician allocation to maintenance jobs"
```
